title: drosera peltata var. foliosa
intro: Drosera peltata var. foliosa, also known as the sundew, is a carnivorous plant species that obtains essential nutrients by capturing and digesting insects. The plant's leaves are covered in sticky droplets of glue-like substance called mucilage, which is responsible for capturing prey. The medicinal properties of Drosera peltata var. foliosa include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the sundew is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and to soothe digestive problems, and its health benefits include promoting immunity and reducing inflammation.
